titlestmt.author: Compiled by RMC Staff

titlepage.titleproper: Guide to the Helen Finch Smith Papers,[ca.1788]-1974.
titlepage.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections Cornell University Library

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

titlepage.date: © 2008 Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library

did.head: DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARY
did.repository: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library
did.abstract: Manuscript, typed, mimeographed and printed material derived from family and genealogical research; local, county, court, church and cemetery records; and printed local and family history, primarily in southern and central New York State and nearby Pennsylvania counties. Includes research notes and correspondence concerning Cayuga, Schuyler, Tompkins, Madison, Chenango, Tioga and Chemung Counties, New York.

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Helen Finch Smith was a genealogist.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Manuscript, typed, mimeographed and printed material derived from family and genealogical research; local, county, court, church and cemetery records; and printed local and family history, primarily in southern and central New York State and nearby Pennsylvania counties. Includes research notes and correspondence concerning Cayuga, Schuyler, Tompkins, Madison, Chenango, Tioga and Chemung Counties, New York.

unittitle.extref: Link to a copy of the finding aid. (PDF file)

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Helen Finch Smith papers, #3777. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
